title: Operating Model
description: A configurable go-to-market / selling motion the marketplace runs: b2b, b2c, c2c, d2c, b2b2c, or p2p (peer-to-peer). A Marketplace ENABLES a subset of Operating Models; an Offer is sold UNDER exactly one. Motions are configuration, not code — enabling or disabling one is flipping a value (`enabled`), never a rewrite. The motion fixes the seller/buyer Identity kinds at the boundary, which is what lets the same platform serve many models at once.

motion.description: b2b | b2c | c2c | d2c | b2b2c | p2p — the configured selling motion.

sellerKind.description: Identity kind on the sell side: organization | person | agent | product.

buyerKind.description: Identity kind on the buy side: organization | person | agent.

enabled.description: Whether this motion is switched on for the marketplace right now (the configuration knob).
